text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Post-Operative Assessment / Pre-Clinical Trial Initiation

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: Medical Oncology
VISIT TYPE: Established Patient Office Visit

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Mr. Raymond Kowalski is a 59-year-old male with metastatic KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma presenting for post-operative follow-up approximately 4 weeks after laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy. He is here to discuss surgical pathology results, current disease status, and timing of enrollment onto the investigational KRAS G12D inhibitor clinical trial.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Kowalski is a 59-year-old former smoker (20 pack-year history, quit approximately 8 years ago) who was initially diagnosed with Stage IV sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma approximately 18 months ago. His disease was characterized by synchronous bilobar hepatic metastases at diagnosis (4 lesions, largest 3.8 cm in segment VI). Molecular profiling demonstrated KRAS p.G12D mutation (VAF 42.3%), TP53 p.R248W, APC truncating mutation, SMAD4 p.R361H, and PIK3CA p.E545K, with a low TMB of 6.2 mut/Mb and MSS status. Given RAS mutation, anti-EGFR therapy was never a consideration.

He received first-line mFOLFOX6 plus Avastin for 12 cycles, achieving a solid partial response in the liver (hepatic lesions shrank by 45-55% individually) and moderate reduction in the primary sigmoid mass. He developed dose-limiting Grade 3 oxali-induced peripheral neuropathy after cycle 9 (requiring Eloxatin dose reduction to 65 mg/m2), as well as progressive Avastin-related hypertension requiring escalation to triple antihypertensive therapy (lisinopril, amlodipine, HCTZ). Following completion of 12 induction cycles, he transitioned to maintenance LV5FU2 plus bev per the OPTIMOX1 strategy.

At approximately 10 months from diagnosis, he developed radiographic progression with new bilateral pulmonary nodules and hepatic lesion enlargement on maintenance therapy. CT-guided biopsy of the right upper lobe pulmonary nodule confirmed metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma (CDX2+, CK20+, TTF-1 negative), excluding primary lung malignancy. He was transitioned to second-line FOLFIRI plus ramu. UGT1A1 genotyping revealed *1/*28 heterozygosity (intermediate metabolizer), prompting a reduced starting Camptosar dose of 150 mg/m2 (subsequently further reduced to 120 mg/m2 after a Grade 3 diarrhea/neutropenia hospitalization after cycle 6). He achieved an early partial response at 4 cycles followed by stable disease at 8 cycles on reduced-dose FOLFIRI plus ramu.

Approximately 17 months into his disease course, he developed intermittent partial colonic obstruction and hematochezia from the primary sigmoid tumor, along with a desire for pre-trial surgical staging. He underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with primary anastomosis, which was uncomplicated (EBL 120 mL, no peritoneal disease identified intraoperatively). Avastin and Cyramza had been held preoperatively per protocol. He is now approximately 4 weeks post-surgery and is recovering well. He has been accepted onto an investigational protocol examining a KRAS G12D inhibitor in combination with Mektovi (a MEK inhibitor) at the nearby NCI-designated cancer center, and today's visit serves in part to confirm readiness to enroll.

Today, Mr. Kowalski reports feeling generally improved since surgery. The obstruction symptoms and rectal bleeding have resolved completely. He denies abdominal pain, fever, wound complications, or changes in bowel habits (now formed stools 1-2 times daily, expected pattern following sigmoid resection). He notes mild residual fingertip numbness bilaterally, consistent with his residual Grade 1 oxali-induced neuropathy, which he describes as stable and not limiting his function. He reports fatigue at a 3/10 level, improved from the peri-operative period. He has resumed light activity and walking. He is eating well with no nausea or weight loss.

His blood pressure at home has been running in the 140-150s/90s range on his current antihypertensive regimen (lisinopril 20 mg daily, amlodipine 10 mg daily, hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg daily). No headaches, visual changes, or chest pain.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Fatigue 3/10, improved from recent nadir. No fever, chills, or night sweats. Appetite good, weight stable post-operatively.
Pulmonary: Mild exertional dyspnea on climbing more than 2 flights of stairs, unchanged from prior to surgery; no pleuritic chest pain or hemoptysis.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema. Blood pressure as noted above.
Gastrointestinal: Surgical incisions healing well. Bowel function normalized to 1-2 formed stools daily. No rectal bleeding since surgery. No nausea or vomiting.
Genitourinary: No dysuria or hematuria. No changes in urinary frequency. Mild hesitancy (baseline, consistent with BPH-type symptoms previously noted).
Neurological: Persistent bilateral distal fingertip numbness (Grade 1 residual neuropathy), stable and not worsening. No gait instability on flat surfaces; mild unsteadiness on very uneven terrain. No headaches or visual disturbances.
Musculoskeletal: No bone pain or joint swelling.
Skin: Grade 1 post-surgical alopecia. Surgical wound sites well-healed per patient report.
Psychiatric: Patient reports mild anxiety around trial enrollment and prognosis, but is in good spirits and motivated. Family support strong.

---

MEDICATIONS
1. Lisinopril 20 mg orally daily (hypertension, bev-related)
2. Amlodipine 10 mg orally daily (hypertension, Avastin-related)
3. Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg orally daily (hypertension)
4. Atorvastatin 20 mg orally daily (hyperlipidemia)
5. Metformin 1000 mg orally twice daily (type 2 diabetes mellitus)
6. Duloxetine 60 mg orally daily (oxali-induced peripheral neuropathy, neuropathic pain)
7. Ondansetron 8 mg orally as needed (nausea, PRN)
8. Loperamide 2 mg orally as needed (diarrhea, PRN)
-- Avastin and Cyramza held preoperatively; FOLFIRI held preoperatively; all systemic therapy on hold pending trial enrollment.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital Signs: Temperature 36.9°C, Heart Rate 78 bpm (regular), Blood Pressure 142/92 mmHg (right arm, seated), Respiratory Rate 16/min, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 74.1 kg (down approximately 2 kg from pre-operative baseline of 76.3 kg), BMI 26.6 kg/m2.
Performance Status: ECOG 1.

General: Well-appearing male in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Appearance consistent with his stated age. No cachexia.

Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Sclerae anicteric. Conjunctivae without pallor. Oral mucosa moist; no mucositis. No oral thrush.

Neck: No cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary lymphadenopathy appreciated. Thyroid non-enlarged.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. S1 and S2 normal. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No lower extremity pitting edema bilaterally.

Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi. No dullness to percussion at the bases.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Well-healing laparoscopic port sites without erythema, induration, or drainage. Three small trocar site scars and one slightly larger extraction site scar in the left lower quadrant, all healing appropriately. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly appreciated on palpation. No ascites. Normoactive bowel sounds. No tenderness on deep palpation of the liver edge. No palpable abdominal masses.

Extremities: No clubbing. Trace bilateral lower extremity edema (1+, symmetric; likely residual from peri-operative period). Peripheral pulses 2+ bilaterally.

Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Mild bilateral decreased light touch sensation at the distal fingertips (Grade 1); plantar sensation mildly diminished bilaterally at the toes. Motor strength 5/5 throughout upper and lower extremities. Gait steady on flat surface; mildly cautious on heel-to-toe tandem walk but no frank ataxia. Deep tendon reflexes 2+ and symmetric.

Skin: No jaundice, rash, or petechiae. Mild, diffuse scalp alopecia (Grade 1). No palmar-plantar erythema.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
(Results from visit today and recent post-operative labs)

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5.8 x 10^9/L (within normal limits; recovery from prior nadir)
- Hemoglobin: 11.4 g/dL (mild anemia, improved from immediate post-operative 10.1 g/dL)
- Hematocrit: 34.1%
- Platelets: 312 x 10^9/L
- ANC: 3.4 x 10^9/L (adequate)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L
- Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L (important given HCTZ; repleted last month)
- Creatinine: 1.09 mg/dL
- eGFR: 72 mL/min/1.73m2 (stable chronic kidney disease stage 2, consistent with prior values)
- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL
- ALT: 28 U/L
- AST: 32 U/L
- Alkaline phosphatase: 114 U/L (mildly elevated, likely hepatic metastases; stable)
- Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
- Total protein: 6.8 g/dL
- Glucose: 118 mg/dL (fasting; well-controlled per recent HbA1c 7.2%)

Tumor Markers:
- CEA: 48.2 ng/mL (decreased from pre-operative 58.8 ng/mL; decline likely reflects contribution of primary tumor removal)
- CA 19-9: 162 U/mL (decreased from pre-operative 189 U/mL)

Coagulation: INR 1.0, PTT 28 seconds (normal, pre-trial requirement met)

Urinalysis: No proteinuria (important pre-trial assessment; urine protein/creatinine ratio 0.12)

Thyroid function: TSH 2.4 mIU/L (normal)

---

IMAGING RESULTS
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (performed approximately 8 weeks ago, after cycle 8 of FOLFIRI plus ramu, prior to surgical planning): Stable pulmonary nodules (right upper lobe 7 mm, right lower lobe 5 mm, left upper lobe 5 mm). Stable hepatic lesions (segment VI 2.0 x 1.7 cm, segment II 0.9 x 0.8 cm, segment IV 1.1 x 0.9 cm; segment VII lesion not visualized). Primary sigmoid mass 2.7 x 2.1 cm at that time -- now resected.

No new restaging imaging has been performed since surgery (expected per trial protocol; new baseline imaging to be obtained at time of trial enrollment within the next 2-3 weeks).

---

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REVIEW
Final pathology from laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy reviewed in detail today:
- Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of sigmoid colon, ypT3N1b (post-chemotherapy treated)
- Histologic tumor response grade: Grade 2 -- partial response with greater than 10% but less than 50% viable tumor cells remaining, indicating moderate but incomplete cytoreduction from prior systemic therapy
- Proximal and distal margins: negative
- Circumferential radial margin: 2.1 mm (negative, though somewhat narrow)
- Lymph nodes: 2 of 13 sampled nodes positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma (deposits 0.4 cm and 0.6 cm)
- Lymphovascular invasion: present
- Perineural invasion: present
- No perforation
- Pathologic restaging: ypT3N1bM1b (Stage IVB, AJCC 8th edition, given ongoing hepatic and pulmonary metastases)

Notably, the finding of 2 positive mesenteric lymph nodes despite partial tumor response and 13 total nodes sampled is consistent with the aggressive biology of SMAD4-mutant, KRAS G12D-driven colorectal cancer. The partial histologic response (grade 2) is encouraging but not a complete pathologic response.

---

ASSESSMENT

Mr. Raymond Kowalski is a 59-year-old male with Stage IVB (ypT3N1bM1b) KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma with persistent bilobar hepatic metastases and bilateral pulmonary nodules (biopsy-confirmed metastatic CRC). He has an 18-month disease history characterized by:

1. Initial partial response to mFOLFOX6 plus Avastin x12 cycles (first line), complicated by Grade 3 oxali neuropathy and bev-associated hypertension
2. Disease progression at 10 months on maintenance LV5FU2/Avastin with new lung metastases
3. Partial response then stable disease on FOLFIRI plus Cyramza (second line), complicated by Grade 3 diarrhea/neutropenia hospitalization requiring Camptosar dose reductions
4. Laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy 4 weeks ago for local symptom control (obstruction, hematochezia) and pre-trial staging
5. Post-operative recovery progressing well; surgically fit for clinical trial enrollment

He has been accepted onto an investigational protocol evaluating a KRAS G12D-specific inhibitor in combination with bini (MEK inhibitor) for KRAS G12D-mutant colorectal cancer. This combination rationale is based on known adaptive MAPK pathway reactivation seen with single-agent KRAS inhibition. Patient meets eligibility: ECOG 0-1 (currently ECOG 1), prior 5-FU and oxali-based therapy, no prior KRAS-targeted therapy, adequate organ function confirmed today.

Prognosis: Discussed with patient in honest and compassionate terms. Median overall survival for KRAS-mutant metastatic CRC is in the range of 18-24 months from diagnosis; Mr. Kowalski is at 18 months and maintains good functional status (ECOG 1) with meaningful disease control. The investigational KRAS G12D-directed trial represents the most promising next step given his molecular profile. We acknowledge that data for KRAS G12D inhibitors in CRC are more limited than in lung cancer, but early signals suggest some activity, and the combination with MEK inhibition may improve outcomes. Goals of care remain aggressive, consistent with patient's expressed wishes. Advance directive is on file.

Ongoing comorbidities and toxicity management:
- Hypertension (Avastin/ramu-related, now Grade 2 post-operative with systemic therapy held): 142/92 mmHg on triple antihypertensives. Will continue current regimen; nephrology co-management ongoing.
- Residual Grade 1 oxali-induced peripheral neuropathy: stable on duloxetine 60 mg daily. No further intervention needed at this time.
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus: well-controlled (HbA1c 7.2%); metformin continued.
- Mild post-operative anemia: Hgb 11.4 g/dL, trending up; no transfusion indicated. Iron studies to be sent if not recovering by next visit.

---

PLAN

1. CLINICAL TRIAL ENROLLMENT: Confirm final eligibility and complete pre-trial baseline studies (new CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast for baseline imaging within 2-3 weeks of trial start; ECG; LVEF assessment by echocardiogram per protocol; repeat CEA/CA 19-9). Trial coordinator to contact patient this week with scheduling. Anticipate trial start approximately 5-6 weeks post-operatively (minimum 4-6 week washout from surgery per protocol; current healing satisfactory).

2. SYSTEMIC THERAPY ON HOLD: FOLFIRI plus ramu held since pre-operative period. Will not resume; transitioning directly to investigational protocol. No bridging chemotherapy planned.

3. TUMOR MARKERS: CEA 48.2 ng/mL and CA 19-9 162 U/mL today -- both have declined somewhat compared to pre-operative values, which may partially reflect the contribution of resecting the primary tumor mass. These will serve as new functional baselines going into the trial, though with metastatic disease in liver and lungs driving the bulk of marker production.

4. HYPERTENSION MANAGEMENT: Continue lisinopril 20 mg, amlodipine 10 mg, and HCTZ 25 mg daily. Blood pressure 142/92 mmHg today -- improved off bev/Cyramza. Will reassess when anti-VEGF exposure resumes on trial (if applicable). Continue nephrology co-management.

5. PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY: Duloxetine 60 mg daily continued. Grade 1 residual neuropathy stable. Recheck at each trial visit. Unlikely to worsen further given Eloxatin has been discontinued.

6. DIABETES: Continue metformin 1000 mg twice daily. Glucose and HbA1c monitoring per primary care, coordinated with trial hepatic toxicity monitoring (metformin may need to be held for contrast studies per institutional protocol).

7. ANEMIA: Mild post-operative anemia (Hgb 11.4 g/dL). Check iron studies and reticulocyte count. If iron deficient, initiate oral ferrous sulfate. Re-check CBC in 4 weeks at trial baseline visit.

8. FOLLOW-UP: Patient to return in 5-6 weeks for trial enrollment confirmation visit and start of investigational protocol. Earlier if any new symptoms, wound concerns, or blood pressure issues. Trial-specific follow-up schedule will follow protocol requirements.

9. ADVANCE DIRECTIVE: On file. Patient has identified his wife as healthcare proxy. Goals of care: aggressive treatment per patient's expressed wishes, with ongoing honest communication about prognosis and disease trajectory.

Time spent: approximately 55 minutes, including comprehensive chart review, discussion of surgical pathology, prognosis counseling, and clinical trial consent discussions. Greater than 50% of visit time spent in counseling and coordination.

Electronically signed by Medical Oncology Attending
